reason: The missing hints affect only the TOC navigation section (s1). Two mitigating factors reduce the impact to low: (1) The s1 section body text already contains the topic labels (`:ref:\`executeQuery-label\``, `:ref:\`insert_update_delete\``, etc.), so full-text search over section content would still surface s1 when users query those topics. (2) This knowledge file only contains sections s1–s6, covering basic implementation and 簡易検索 topics. The actual implementation guidance for executeQuery, insert/update/delete, object-select, and map-search is absent from this file entirely. Even if s1 is retrieved via these hints, users would receive only a TOC pointer — not actionable implementation content. Therefore, adding these hints to s1 would slightly improve TOC retrieval but would not enable the agent to answer the corresponding implementation questions. The finding is legitimate but does not warrant blocking.

findings_assessment.justification: The s1 section is a TOC-only entry. Its body already contains the missing topic labels as :ref: cross-references, making them available to full-text search even without hint coverage. More critically, the knowledge file has no sections covering executeQuery, insert/update/delete, object-select, or map-search implementations — so retrieving s1 via these hints would only surface a navigation list, not implementation guidance. The fix was not applied by Phase E (the output JSON is byte-for-byte identical to the Phase D output), but the practical consequence is negligible given the above.
